req.title: Approve leave request — variant 3
req.statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) a manager approving an assigned leave request (happy path, EP), (2) a non-assignee manager attempting approval (403, NEG), (3) a request already actioned receiving a second approval attempt (409, STC/NEG), (4) approval on the exact 5th business-day boundary before auto-escalation (BVA), and (5) webhook emission verification (DT — action x outcome matrix). Five tests provide EP, NEG, STC, BVA, and DT coverage. Each test uses fixtures for auth tokens, request seeding, and HTTP client; placeholders keep executable blocks under 40 lines.

tests.name: test_manager_approves_assigned_request_returns_approved_state
preconditions: Leave request exists with status=pending and is assigned to this manager
Employee account is active and has a notification channel configured
expected: Employee notification is recorded in notification service
Audit log entry created for this approval action
Webhook event with type=leave_request.approved is emitted
trace: R-WEB-064: request state transitions to approved
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_approves_assigned_request_returns_approved_state(
    api_client, manager_token, pending_leave_request, webhook_listener
):
    request_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    headers = <auth_header>(manager_token)

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{request_id}/approve",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "approved"

    notifications = <get_notifications>(employee_id=pending_leave_request["employee_id"])
    assert any(n["event"] == "leave_approved" for n in notifications)

    audit = <get_audit_log>(resource_id=request_id)
    assert any(a["action"] == "approve" for a in audit)

    events = webhook_listener.received_events(event_type="leave_request.approved")
    assert len(events) >= 1
    assert events[0]["payload"]["request_id"] == request_id

tests.name: test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403
preconditions: Two manager accounts exist: manager_a (assignee) and manager_b (non-assignee)
Leave request is assigned exclusively to manager_a with status=pending
expected: Response body contains error code FORBIDDEN_NOT_ASSIGNEE
trace: R-WEB-064: error pathway — 403 if manager not the assignee
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403(
    api_client, manager_b_token, pending_leave_request_assigned_to_a, webhook_listener
):
    request_id = pending_leave_request_assigned_to_a["id"]
    headers = <auth_header>(manager_b_token)

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{request_id}/approve",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "FORBIDDEN_NOT_ASSIGNEE"

    current = <get_leave_request>(api_client, request_id)
    assert current["status"] == "pending"

    audit = <get_audit_log>(resource_id=request_id)
    assert not any(a["action"] == "approve" for a in audit)

    events = webhook_listener.received_events(event_type="leave_request.approved")
    assert len(events) == 0

tests.name: test_double_approve_same_request_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated user has role:manager and is the assignee
Leave request exists with status=approved (already actioned)
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve a second time with the same manager JWT
expected: Response body contains error code ALREADY_ACTIONED
trace: R-WEB-064: error pathway — 409 if already actioned
R-WEB-064: state transition from approved -> approve attempt is invalid
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_double_approve_same_request_returns_409(
    api_client, manager_token, already_approved_leave_request, webhook_listener
):
    request_id = already_approved_leave_request["id"]
    headers = <auth_header>(manager_token)

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{request_id}/approve",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "ALREADY_ACTIONED"

    current = <get_leave_request>(api_client, request_id)
    assert current["status"] == "approved"

    audit = <get_audit_log>(resource_id=request_id)
    approve_entries = [a for a in audit if a["action"] == "approve"]
    assert len(approve_entries) == 1

    events = webhook_listener.received_events(event_type="leave_request.approved")
    assert len(events) == 1

tests.name: test_approve_on_5th_business_day_before_auto_escalation
preconditions: Authenticated user has role:manager and is the assignee
Leave request was assigned exactly 5 business days ago (boundary — last valid action window)
Auto-escalation job has NOT yet run for this cycle
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve with valid manager JWT within the 5th business day
expected: Request is NOT escalated to hr-admin
Audit entry records approval timestamp within the 5-business-day window
trace: R-WEB-064: manager must act within 5 business days
R-WEB-064: BVA upper boundary — day 5 is still valid, day 6 triggers escalation
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def test_approve_on_5th_business_day_before_auto_escalation(
    api_client, manager_token, webhook_listener
):
    request_id = <seed_leave_request_aged_n_business_days>(
        api_client, manager_token, business_days=5
    )
    headers = <auth_header>(manager_token)

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{request_id}/approve",
        headers=headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "approved"
    assert body.get("escalated_to") is None

    audit = <get_audit_log>(resource_id=request_id)
    approval_entry = next((a for a in audit if a["action"] == "approve"), None)
    assert approval_entry is not None
    assert <within_business_day_window>(approval_entry["timestamp"], window_days=5)

    events = webhook_listener.received_events(event_type="leave_request.approved")
    assert len(events) >= 1

tests.name: test_approve_emits_correct_webhook_payload
preconditions: Authenticated user has role:manager and is the assignee
Webhook listener is subscribed for leave_request.approved events
actions: Capture webhook event received by listener
expected: Webhook payload contains correct request_id, employee_id, manager_id, and timestamp
Webhook is delivered within 10 seconds of the API response
trace: R-WEB-064: action must emit a webhook event for external consumers
DT: approve action x webhook outcome — valid manager, assigned request
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time

WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 10

def test_approve_emits_correct_webhook_payload(
    api_client, manager_token, pending_leave_request, webhook_listener
):
    request_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    employee_id = pending_leave_request["employee_id"]
    manager_id = pending_leave_request["assignee_id"]
    headers = <auth_header>(manager_token)

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{request_id}/approve",
        headers=headers,
    )
    approval_time = time.time()

    assert resp.status_code == 200

    events = webhook_listener.wait_for_event(
        event_type="leave_request.approved",
        timeout=WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
    )
    assert events is not None, "Webhook event not received within timeout"
    payload = events["payload"]
    assert payload["request_id"] == request_id
    assert payload["employee_id"] == employee_id
    assert payload["manager_id"] == manager_id
    assert "timestamp" in payload
    assert (time.time() - approval_time) <= WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
